metadata.title: Proof-of-personhood to programmable identity: Worldcoin/World, eIDAS 2.0 EUDI wallets, the Digital Euro, EU Chat Control, and the UK Online Safety Act
metadata.status: facts graded individually; the 'convergence into one identity+payment+scanning rail' is a labeled structural interpretation, not asserted intent
metadata.frame: Five tracks that are usually reported separately but r-build the SAME stack from different angles: a biometric 'are-you-human' credential (Worldcoin/World), a state identity wallet (eIDAS 2.0 EUDI), state programmable money (Digital Euro), message-scanning + mandatory age verification (EU Chat Control / CSAR), and platform age-gating with ID (UK Online Safety Act). The shared public justification is child-protection / anti-fraud / anti-deepfake; the shared structural output is a unified, attributable identity layer bound to payments and communications. Overlay; excluded from the formal proofs; ties to [[reject-age-verification]] and age-verification-abolition.

worldcoin_world.what: OpenAI co-founder Sam Altman's World (formerly Worldcoin), built by Tools for Humanity: iris-scanning 'Orb' issues a 'World ID' proof-of-personhood + WLD token.
worldcoin_world.stated_rationale: Altman: because AI can now imitate humans online, the world needs 'proof of personhood' to tell humans from bots. status: fact (his stated motive).
worldcoin_world.scale: claims >18M verified across ~160 countries; ~7,000 Orbs across six US cities by Apr 2025. status: contested (self-reported).
worldcoin_world.regulatory_rejection: banned/suspended/investigated for biometric-consent and data-law violations in Brazil, Spain, Hong Kong, Portugal, Germany, Kenya, South Korea, Colombia, Indonesia (May 2025), Philippines (Oct 2025, 'exploitation of vulnerable populations'), Thailand (Nov 2025, ordered deletion). status: fact.
worldcoin_world.us_pivot_partnerships: while blocked abroad, expanding in the US; Apr 2026 announced World ID verification partnerships with Tinder, Zoom and Docusign (anti-deepfake/anti-scam framing). status: fact.
worldcoin_world.reading: a private, token-incentivised global biometric registry positioning to become the 'human' login layer for AI-era platforms - i.e. the same node (Altman/OpenAI) that built the deepfake problem also sells the identity 'cure'. status: contested interpretation.

eidas2_eudi_wallet.what: EU Digital Identity Wallet under eIDAS 2.0, Regulation (EU) 2024/1183 (in force May 2024). All 27 member states must offer every citizen/resident a wallet by Dec 2026.
eidas2_eudi_wallet.scope_creep: binds national eID to driving licences, diplomas, professional qualifications and BANK ACCOUNTS in one wallet; selective-disclosure age proofs; EU Age Verification Blueprint open-sourced 15 Apr 2026, piloted by FR/DK/GR/IT/ES/CY/IE. status: fact.
eidas2_eudi_wallet.security_caveat: the EU age-verification reference app was reportedly hacked/spoofed within ~2 minutes; specs still incomplete near the deadline. status: contested (per security reporting) - reinforces the age-verification-futility result.
eidas2_eudi_wallet.reading: the wallet is the keystone: once identity, credentials and bank account share one credential, every later 'age check' or 'payment' can be an authenticated, logged identity event. status: contested interpretation.

digital_euro.what: ECB retail CBDC. European Council approved direction Dec 2025; European Parliament vote expected Jun 2026; ECB targeting technical standards summer 2026, pilot H2-2027, possible first issuance ~2029.
digital_euro.design_tension: marketed as 'digital cash' (privacy, offline); but a central-bank ledger is programmable and identity-linkable by construction. status: fact (timeline) + contested (privacy claims).
digital_euro.reading: supplies the programmable-money half of an identity+money rail; pairs naturally with the EUDI wallet. status: contested interpretation. Cross-ref macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail (the US private-stablecoin version of the same rail).

eu_chat_control_csar.what: Child Sexual Abuse Regulation ('Chat Control'): originally mandated client-side scanning of private messages.
eu_chat_control_csar.the_pivot: after years of opposition, on 31 Oct 2025 Denmark dropped MANDATORY scanning (Germany + others refused); Council position adopted 26 Nov 2025 removes the forced-scan/break-encryption mandate BUT (a) legitimises 'voluntary' scanning of non-E2EE messages and (b) ADDS mandatory age verification for communication providers. Trilogues scheduled Feb/May/Jun 2026. status: fact.
eu_chat_control_csar.reading: the surveillance goal survived the privacy 'win' by switching levers - from scan-everyone to identify-everyone (age verification kills anonymity); the child-protection frame is the constant. status: contested interpretation.

uk_online_safety_act.what: Age-verification duties live 25 Jul 2025; Ofcom requires 'highly effective' checks (facial estimation, credit card, digital ID); fines up to GBP 18M or 10% of global revenue + senior-exec criminal liability.
uk_online_safety_act.revealed_preference: immediate VPN surge - Proton +1,400-1,800% UK signups ('levels usually associated with civil unrest'), NordVPN +1,000%; half the top-10 UK App Store apps on launch day were VPN/ID apps; Ofcom concedes it cannot stop VPNs and floats action against VPN-promotion. status: fact.
uk_online_safety_act.reading: mass circumvention shows the public treats it as surveillance, not safety; the enforcement reflex (target VPNs next) reveals the goal is attributability, not merely blocking minors. status: contested interpretation.

the_common_pattern: Each track is sold on an unimpeachable, parent-sympathetic harm (CSAM, deepfakes, minors, fraud). Each delivers a piece of one stack: a 'human' credential (World), a state identity wallet (EUDI), programmable money (Digital Euro), and age/ID gates on speech (Chat Control, OSA). The provable structural fact is the STACK; specific coordinating INTENT across actors is NOT asserted (graded, quarantined). See digitalid-orchestration-real-incentive for the incentive analysis.
